steps.title: Turn the conditions into two rows
steps.content: For a linear function, $g(x+1)-g(x)=5$ means every time $x$ increases by $1$, the function value increases by $5$. Since $g(3)=11$, the next point is $g(4)=16$.

steps.title: Find the line from the data
steps.content: Using $(3,11)$ and $(4,16)$, the slope is $$m=\frac{16-11}{4-3}=5.$$ Substitute $(3,11)$ into $y=5x+b$: $$11=15+b,$$ so $b=-4$. The equation is $y=5x-4$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: $g(x)=3x+11$ and $g(x)=3x+5$ have slope $3$, so they do not satisfy $g(x+1)-g(x)=5$. $g(x)=5x-15$ has slope $5$, but $g(3)=5(3)-15=0$, not $11$. The function $g(x)=5x-4$ has $g(3)=11$ and increases by $5$ each step.
